desc:
The river bends again in its course, cutting through the lands within the
confines of the limestone ravine. Its route takes its far, to become a tributary
of the Hoarwell. The Hoarwell in turn merges with the Swanfleet at Tharbad,
becoming in turn the river Greyflood which flows to the sea.
name: The Nen-i-Sul

desc:
The lines of grasses here suggest that these fields were once used for growing
crops but have since been fallow. The boundaries of the crops can still be seen
in the pattern of the vegetation that has taken over. The relative bounty of the
land is a result of a slightly elevated land and the presence of a small stream
which winds through the grass. There is no sign of a farm.
name: Fallow Fields

desc:
Grass covers the once orderly lines of fields, suggesting that the area retains
a better than average amount of water than much of the Lone-lands. The lines of
the ancient fields are bounded by low hedgerows. A single hedge also marks the
edge of the drop into the ravine to the west and south.
name: Fallow Fields

desc:
From within this tower you can look out from the castle over the lone-lands and
the south downs. To the north, you have a good view of the nearby trail which
winds just south of the great hedge, which in old times marked the eastern
border of the kingdom of Cardolan. Looking out of the east slit you see the
hedge also, as it cuts its way across the great plains towards the Mitheithel
river in south-western Rhudaur. Beyond the hedge, the plains carry on towards
the Trollshaws, dotted here and there by small collections of wood and bush.
Looking south, the grasslands stretch where sheep farmers still try and eke a
living on the eastern ridges of the south downs. Finally, to the west lies the
great Old East Road, its southern side lined by great thorny bushes for many
miles. The Road passes the foot of mighty Weathertop and crosses the Nen-i-Sul
river, making its way towards Bree-land.
name: In the Tower

desc:
From here you can espy the surroundings to the north - the Lone-lands. You can
see the nearby trail which follows the thorny hedge to the north. Crossing this
trail is a rough track which heads out through the gates and through the
hedgewall further north, the bushes being blacked there. You can only head up
some steps into the remaining tower, or head back down to the castle yard.
name: On the Castle Walls

desc:
The castle walls loom above from all sides within these grounds, with some
stairs leading up to the top of the walls. The stairs have many cracks and holes
in them, some steps missing completely. There is a large stone building over
towards the corner of the yard, with some letters etched in the stone above the
doorway. Some grass grows in patches all around the yard with mud covering the
rest of the land. Further south is the entrance to the castle itself.
name: Castle Yard

desc:
A large iron gate hangs here halfway open with its long sharp ends pointing
towards the ground. The gate creaks loudly and shakes with even the slightest
breeze. The slightest vibrations could bring the gate crashing down. There is a
wooden rail freshly built just outside the gate, a place to tie the horses and
walk through the gate. The surface of the walls around the gate has become
discoloured from the rust that pours off the iron bars. A few bars from the gate
are missing near the bottom from where the rust has eaten all the way through
them.
name: Old Castle Gate

desc:
You are standing in front of the castle gate where the rusty old portcullis lies
shattered on the ground in three pieces. The castle wall is full of holes but
its structure is still pretty stable. Through the gate you can see a dusty
castle yard.
name: Before the Castle Gate

desc:
The plains stretch on endlessly here, grass growing rampant through the untended
years. This place deserves to be called the Lone-lands since hardly anybody
lives in these parts. A castle stands here, although it has seen the best of its
days. The walls are broken and impassable.
name: Plains

desc:
The plains stretch on endlessly here, grass growing rampant through the years.
It is no wonder that this place is called the Lone-lands, nary a soul lives
here. However, here stands a ruined castle, its walls broken but still
impassable to all but the foolhardy. It seems the only building within view.
name: Plains

desc:
The trail following the southern side of the great wall of thorns is heading
roughly from south-east to north-west. It looks to be still in use as the
grasses are flattened along its length. Maybe merchants use this route or even
the famed Rangers of the North, who knows?
name: The Hedge-Trail

desc:
There is no way to cross the barrier of thorns standing to the north, that is
for sure. What was once built and maintained by the marshals of Cardolan stands
even though the realm fell to Angmar and plague. Another remnant is the stony
trail that you stand upon as its cuts its way through the grassy terrain. The
vista to the south-west is of a landscape of grass, dotted here and there by the
odd bush. Further in that distance, the terrain rises and becomes greener as the
fertile South Downs start.
name: The Hedge-Trail

desc:
Walking around in these rough grounds requires great care not to trip and hurt
oneself. It is tiresome and you long for some smoother ground to walk on. A few
of the rocks have seemingly been toppled over quite recently, reminding you that
you are not the first to visit this place.
name: Rough Grounds

desc:
The ground here is strewn with bare rocks dotting the white plains. Slightly
sloping upwards, passage to the west becomes more and more strenuous, as the
grounds also get rougher and the rocks larger. To the north the grass grows
dense and high, and takes on a greyish tone, while it grows more sparse east and
south. A few faint high-pitched shrieks are heard from the sky, and you notice
the shadow of a bird of prey hovering over the plains far southeast.
name: Chalk Plains

desc:
The plains you are walking on are unusually quiet. The vegetation is sparse,
except for the dry grass that grows up to your waist. To your west you can see a
small fortress but otherwise the vista is desolate and dull.
name: Plains

desc:
Endlessly the great hedge grows through the grasslands, the trail which you are
upon doggedly shadowing it. The chalky soil in the surrounding prairie produces
little else but hardy grasses. The wind blows across your face and the only real
break from it is sheltering under the thorns.
name: The Hedge-Trail

desc:
You seem to be on an old trail from the elder days of Arnor. Rocks and cobbles
maintain the track still, but it is hopelessly overgrown as well. However, there
is still signs that it is used, since the grass have been trodden down.
name: The Hedge-Trail

desc:
Close to where the southbound hedge on your west side turns east, the ground is
heavily cracked up by thorns and shallow roots here. The white, hard soil that
dominate the slightly hilly plains to the east and north of here seems to
provide too little nourishment even for the thorns, and they do not grow far in
those directions. Grey and brown grass are the only species that care to grow
there, and no signs of wildlife are obvious except a lone hawk high up in the
northeastern sky.
name: Plains by the Hedge

desc:
The old trail continues, offering to take the traveller northwest or southeast.
The ground is hard and trampled and covered with small pebbles. Judging from the
tracks and the trampled grass of the edges of the trail it is still very much in
use.
name: The Hedge-Trail

desc:
You stir up some dirt as you move and a small animal scurries away into the
grass. Although obviously used, the trail is covered with rocks and branches
from the roadside vegetation stretches over the trail in an attempt to cover it
completely.
name: The Hedge-Trail

desc:
The trail ends and begins here. Once it continued south over a bridge, but that
bridge has been torn down for long. Not much remains of it except a few rotting
planks on the bottom of a steep ravine that emerges from the bushes to the east
and grinds its way southwest to the Nen-i-Sul. Thick and high thorns surround
you on the south and east sides, hiding the trail from the morning sun. Only
some sparse grey grass cares to live here, trying to fend off the thorns.
Impromptu paths have been cleared to the north and east, doubtless since the
collapse of the bridge.
name: End of the Hedge-Trail

desc:
The thick Great Hedge that once protected the lands of Cardolan grows south of
here, while white rolling plains stretch out in all other directions. Nothing
much grows on them except for some short and coarse grey and brown grass, hiding
in the shallow valleys between the low hills. Small dry brooks have dug their
ways south for ages, and now look like wrinkles in an old man's face. Not much
can be seen moving around, as the plains offer little protection for animals.
name: Plains by the Hedge

desc:
The land before you slope slowly away south-westwards, with many small, dry
southbound brooks between tiny rolling hills. The grass is scanty, coarse and
grey, growing mostly in patches between large areas of white hard earth. The
hard earth hurts the feet, but is easy to travel. In all directions these arid
plains continue, with some higher grass only to the north.
name: Chalk Plains

desc:
An old fountain stands in front of the castle, many dead leaves fester in the
still water that is left in the bottom of it. A brick pathway leads around the
fountain from the gate, all the way up to the large entrance to the keep. Grass
and weeds grow through the cracks that line this path. Ivy grows throughout the
area, taking a stranglehold wherever it can.
name: The Castle Square

desc:
Large windows line the walls of this corridor, with huge red curtains hanging
from above. The curtains are of dark red colour and extremely thick. A mural
hangs from the other wall towards the inside of the castle, depicting a battle
of glory for the castle. The corridor leads onward towards the south, with more
large windows framed in thick wood. Many of them have been broken, glass left
lying on the ground. Looking out through the broken windows you see an orchard
in the yard.
name: Corridor

desc:
Along the western side is a large stone arch that leads all the way down the
corridor. A frame made out of thick oak stands within the arches. There are a
few large hooks in the wall on the other side. A broken handle from a large
great sword hangs from one of those iron hooks, its blade long since broken off.
There is a pile of a torn and scorched red cloth lying here.
name: Corridor

desc:
There are a few racks standing here, with some rusty hangers dangling from them.
The racks line up in rows all across the room, with a few clothes hanging from
them. There are some tattered clothes lying on the ground all torn or burnt.
Some clothes hang from the racks closest to the door, their cloth newer and
handled with more care. A small desk stands beside the door, its legs covered
with cobwebs, and a small chair sits behind the desk. The chair itself has no
cobwebs on it, and there are some tracks in the dust from where the chair has
been moved.
name: Cloak Room

desc:
Some dried blood stains the walls of this hallway. There is a door here, and a
large broken bar lying next to it. There are some tattered clothes with
blood-stains on them scattered on the floor. The hallway bends southward where
more objects lie on the ground undisturbed.
name: Hallway

desc:
There is a small broken door to the south with garbage piled in front of it
making progress impossible in that direction. The walls are stained with blood
and tattered clothes are scattered around the room. A wooden beam from the
ceiling lies upon the ground, burnt beyond use.
name: End of Hallway

desc:
A large oak table stands in the middle of this room, with many garnished metal
dinner plates scattered across its top. Remains of candles lie on the table in
pieces while high above hangs a large chandelier with only a few crystal pieces
missing from it. Along the walls are pictures of past rulers of the castle,
their faces long lost from the many slashes taken out on the paintings. A large
door stands just towards the south, with two large knights in armour standing
before it. They have been rusted through, and their hands stand poised, yet the
weapons that they once wielded have been removed from their grasp.
name: Banqueting Hall

desc:
It is dark within this old building, the stone ceiling has been kept intact
throughout all the neglect in this castle. A few rotten cots lie in the corner
of the building with rusted spears sitting beside them. An old desk lies broken
on the floor on the other side of the room. A book rests barely on top of the
slanted desk. There are some words written on top of the dark green book. There
is also a small hole in the back of the building.
name: Old Stone Building

desc:
This is the top of a small set of stairs that wind downward into the darkness
below, with its walls made of a cold rough stone. There are sconces for torches
to hang on the wall made out of a brass mould with carved statues on them, but
no torches remain.
name: Stairs Leading Down

desc:
The stairs from above widen out and reveal a large room containing rough tables
and some instruments of torture. One skeletal body lies on the floor, with many
pieces missing from it. Bite marks line the legs and arms of the former victim
of this place. A cell, isolated by crude bars, is east of here.
name: The Dungeon

desc:
This is where the prisoners of the current occupants of the castle are kept. A
small deep hole is over in one of the corners, with an pungent odour drifting up
from it. A small cot lies on one of the walls with little left of it. The
mattress is torn and scattered about the room. The only way out of this room is
back west, through the iron bars, which though crude, are sturdy.
name: Prison Cell

desc:
The corridor comes to an end here, with a large statue of an unknown soldier
standing here, pointing his sword towards the east. A large mural hangs behind
him depicting some long past battle and great victory. There is a large entrance
to a darkened room here. There are a few broken and rusted swords lying on the
ground here.
name: End of Corridor

desc:
There is a long path leading up to a huge golden coloured throne in this hall.
The throne has many wild animals carved on it. The back is carved to look like a
large bear and snakes curl down the sides. Some red curtains hang draping the
room slightly on both sides of the throne, with two large statues watching over
the throne with their swords in their hands drawn.
name: The Throne-Hall

desc:
This cave is hollowed out below the hole above. The trash in this room is piled
quite high in the bottom, and the acrid smell is very strong. The bedding
material consists of dried bushes and gnawed bones. The only visible exit is to
clamber back up through the hole.
name: The Troll-Home

desc:
This is a low, wide natural cavern. The walls are quite rough and contain many
crevices. The ceiling above contains a hole five feet across, where the process
of erosion have removed the chalky rock. Someone has made an effort to cover
this hole, as a large stone slab has been laid above. A timber post is wedged
into the floor and under this slab, perhaps to lever it open? The floor contains
a hole similar to the one above. The tell-tale signs of claw marks on the walls,
and the strong odour reveal that trolls live here!
name: A Great, Dirty Cave

desc:
This small tunnel must have been half-forgotten, it is such a tight passage.
Maybe it was a sewer in the past, the strong odour certainly suggests this. The
walls of the tunnel bear stone-cladding, but much of this has fallen into
disrepair. Where stone-tiles have fallen into the crawl-space you have even more
difficulty manoeuvring. Lets hope you don't have to retreat as you have no room
to turn back now.
name: A Narrow Crawlway

desc:
Here the rocky passage bends sharply. The timber props here are few but the
rough ceiling looks safe enough. An acrid smell pervades this area and it hangs
in the windless air. The passage continues south into pitch darkness, or west
when it bends north after a short distance. Another small tunnel can be seen
heading east. It is very low and small, and you will have to crawl along it to
pass.
name: Bend in the Long Tunnel

desc:
The tunnel that you are in bends rather sharply here. On first examination there
seems no reason for this to happen. However, looking at the south way of the
tunnel you see that part of a volcanic extrusion has been revealed. The ceiling
here is lacking any support as the props lie rotting in some water. The only
choices are to go east around this rock, or walk north into the dark.
name: Bend in the Long Tunnel

desc:
The great limestone rift, Orchabal's Fissure, starts and ends here. It course
takes it westward, far far beyond ones sight. Though this underground feature
exists in the middle of what was a land controlled by the Dunedain, only a
rudimentary amount of building has taken place. The major feature is the roadway
which is cut into the rock, making an underground path along the chasm, a place
which would be too treacherous for normal travel otherwise. If dwarves had lived
in numbers nearby, generations of dwarves would surely have made a great deal of
fuss about this place, carving intricate designs into the walls, capping stones
and creating stable arches through which to travel. Here there is merely a brass
plaque next to another crack leading south.
name: End of Orchabal's Fissure

desc:
Judging from the columns which pierce the tunnel, this area once saw quite a lot
of water flowing down from the rock above. However, at some stage the flow has
stopped and the stone-structures have dried up, becoming weakened by the lack of
humidity. Many of the ruddy-coloured stalactites have come crashing down, while
a part of the wall has fallen away, forcing you to clamber over.
name: Orchabal's Fissure

desc:
In either direction, there is no sign of light or life. The years of waters
trickling down the limestone have created a colourful spectacle: green and brown
stains of minerals standing out against the lightly coloured stone. The
direction of the fissure is comparatively well behaved in this section, giving
the miners of the passage through the fissure little to do besides flattening
the floor and removing dangerous overhangs.
name: Orchabal's Fissure

desc:
The fissure alternately grows and narrows, while the architects of the tunnel
have made use of this natural route. At times one side of the trail falls away
as a path is cut into one side of the great crack. At other times the ceiling
drops dramatically making it only high enough for a short man to pass through
without stooping.
name: Orchabal's Fissure

desc:
The great underground scar twists in its passage below the surface of the earth.
Exaggerating this, the hewn passage within the fissure crosses from the northern
side of the fissure to the south by means a slightly arched bridge. This bridge
looks rather more unsettling than it really is, its construction having been
further established by the accumulation of minerals which further cement the
span. A large stalactite above the bridge narrows to a point some ten feet
before reaching the span. A smaller stalagmite lies on one side of the bridge.
Perhaps in a thousand years, these two will meet and fuse, providing another
support to the man-made structure.
name: Bridge across the Fissure

desc:
Miles and miles one walks, not seeing any end. Within this great underground
fissure, a man-made tunnel is roughly hewn. The uneven direction of the
limestone crack means that the tunnellers have had to follow its route,
minimising their own lot. At one point a rather large crack from floor to
ceiling provides an obstacle to cross, a slow rain of drops creating a curtain
of dripping water. At another point, several giant-sized boulders lie strewn
across the floor, blocking much of the fissure and creating a strong breeze.
name: Orchabal's Fissure

desc:
A kin of the other chamber, this one does not contain any books, but seems to be
where most of the experiments take place. On the shelves sit trinkets as well as
an assortment of household utensils. A small stove stands beside the eastern
wall, currently unlit. Beside the stove, coals provide the necessary fuel. A
deep recess in the southeast corner of the chamber contains a grating at the
bottom, providing a means of disposal. Reams of paper line the desk which stands
in the northeast corner, while the candle on the centre has burnt low. Notably,
various flasks of strange liquids also stand. Beside the desk is a functional
bed, the sheets turned down and looking rather in need of a wash.
name: Back Laboratory

desc:
The tunnel burrows crudely through the chalky rock. Sturdy timbers hold up the
low ceiling which is crumbly to the touch. The passageway seems hundreds of
paces in length though it is difficult to judge when you cannot see both ends
simultaneously. The air is very dry and chalk-dust is brought up by your
footsteps. No ventilating breeze dispels the cloud, which eventually settles.
You have no choice but to travel north towards a bend which is just visible, or
to head in the opposite direction.
name: Long Tunnel

desc:
This is the end of the chalk-hewn tunnel, supported by stout wooden props. It
ends abruptly at a dressed stone wall. A torch bracket is fixed to the wall, but
the wood it contains is unlit. The tunnel heads north into the darkness.
name: End of the Long Tunnel

desc:
The walls of this chamber are close together, yet the walls are adorned with
fine pictures. There is even one full length picture standing there depicting a
battle between the forces of Cardolan and a group of Hill-men from the east. The
only way out seems to be through a panel above your head.
name: A Secret Chamber

desc:
Huge thorny bushes tower higher than the tallest man, surround and smother all
space not actually filled by their interlaced thorny withes, and tear
mercilessly at your clothing and flesh. The ground is hard, stony, and covered
with thorny debris. A vague soft rustling can be heard now and again, and the
characteristic squeak of rats.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You push your way into a stand of gigantically tall grey weeds which stand like
spears, pointing skywards. They rise to heights which dwarf even you and their
stems grow as thick as your wrist. `These are not ordinary weeds', you think to
yourself and you even imagine you hear a low humming noise among them.
name: A Tall Stand of Weeds

desc:
You push your way into a stand of gigantically tall grey weeds which stand like
spears, pointing skywards. They rise to heights which dwarf even you and their
stems grow as thick as your wrist. `These are not ordinary weeds', you think to
yourself and you even imagine you hear a low humming noise among them.
name: A Tall Stand of Weeds

desc:
This is flat, dryish land which is covered by brown thorny bushes for miles
around. Their creepers seem to wave strangely in the flat air and to tear
maliciously at you. There is no sign that this land is inhabited by anyone.
name: Lonely Scrubland

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. This
trail of barren plains turns northward as a stand of weeds and a line of brush
block its path east or west.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You wander into a patch of shrubs dominated by dark gnarled trees whos warped
and twisted branches stretch all around, forming a canopy which blocks out all
external light. As you move, you step on old blackened leaves which crunch
loudly as you tread upon them and dark crows scatter from their shelter as you
approach.
name: Dark Clump of Trees

desc:
You wander into a patch of shrubs dominated by dark gnarled trees whos warped
and twisted branches stretch all around, forming a canopy which blocks out all
external light. As you move, you step on old blackened leaves which crunch
loudly as you tread upon them and dark crows scatter from their shelter as you
approach.
name: Dark Clump of Trees

desc:
The land here slopes gently downward toward the south. It is ill-drained and
barely fertile; the vegetation consists of clumps of matted grass and scattered
stands of thorny trees and vines. There is little sign that this land has ever
been inhabited by man, elf, orc or beast. Occasionally something will squeak in
the bushes, or a lonely bird-call will be heard.
name: Thorny Scrubland

desc:
The chalky plains you are standing on continue north and west for several miles.
The small patch of red flowers upon a small hill nearby must be close relatives
to Seregon, the blood flower that grew on Amon Rudh. The hill will look drenched
in blood in the mornings, shine like a red carpet during mid-day, and glow like
a slow burning fire when the sun sets.
name: Chalk Plains

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: Scrubland

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You push your way into a stand of gigantically tall grey weeds which stand like
spears, pointing skywards. They rise to heights which dwarf even you and their
stems grow as thick as your wrist. `These are not ordinary weeds', you think to
yourself and you even imagine you hear a low humming noise among them.
name: A Tall Stand of Weeds

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. Within
this large expanse of barren land, the only signs of plant life you see are a
tall group of weeds growing to the west.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. Weeds
surround you on two sides, and brush on the third as the patch of clear land you
were following ends.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, throw this sea of thorns.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. Here,
the plains are swallowed up by the brush which assaults it on all sides save
south.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. The thorny creepers seem to move, to wave,
to catch at you, to strike at you. The vegetation seems not merely difficult -
but malevolent.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You push your way into a stand of gigantically tall grey weeds which stand like
spears, pointing skywards. They rise to heights which dwarf even you and their
stems grow as thick as your wrist. `These are not ordinary weeds', you think to
yourself and you even imagine you hear a low humming noise among them.
name: A Tall Stand of Weeds

desc:
You push your way into a stand of gigantically tall grey weeds which stand like
spears, pointing skywards. They rise to heights which dwarf even you and their
stems grow as thick as your wrist. `These are not ordinary weeds', you think to
yourself and you even imagine you hear a low humming noise among them.
name: A Tall Stand of Weeds

desc:
Sparse brown grass grows here among occasional thorns and some kind of grey
weedy plants. Further east where there is no more of this white unforgiving
earth, these grey plants shoot in the sky and grow several yards high. West,
north, and south, white rolling plains stretch out for several miles, until the
vegetation grows denser and paints a grey-green streak just below the horizon,
occasionally tainted by the darker brown colour of an east-westbound road
climbing some small hills to the northwest.
name: Chalk Plains

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns. There are a few muddy pools here in what is apparently a dry creek bed.
name: Thorny Brush

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. You
follow the dry plains until they vanish into a grove of shadowy trees and giant
grey weeds.
name: The Flats

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it.
Standing on a large patch of dried earth, you can see nothing but grey plains
except for a stand of evil-looking trees which jut out to the north.
name: The Flats

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You find yourself amid a stand of thorny bushes which tear your clothes and skin
as you try to wade through it. It is at times so thick and dense that you wonder
whether you will become stuck in it. But, fortunately, your constant pain pays
off as your limbs move you, albeit slow and painfully, through this sea of
thorns. `Where has the road gone?', you ask yourself as you scan the brush for
any sign of the road, but unless your eyes decieve you, you find no sign of the
road. It is as if the Old East Road never was.
name: In the Brush

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. This
dried clump of earth is in the middle of a field of shrubs and a dark stand of
trees.
name: The Flats

desc:
You are standing upon one of many plots of dry, broken earth which make up these
plains. Around you grow evil looking shrubs and weeds that grow taller than
you'd think possible. Small shoots of brown grass try to grow within the cracks
of the dried earth which creaks like old floorboards when you walk on it. These
flats stretch away to the north and west, bordered to the east by a band of
shrubs.
name: The Flats

desc:
There are a few tapestries hanging from the walls in this room, their worn
pictures indistinct as they have faded so much. Many more tapestries lie on the
ground below them seeming to act as an entrance mat. Two large suits of armour
stand near the entrance of a room towards the west, their metal dull and rusted.
A cloakroom lies to the south, while halls lead off east and west.
name: Entrance to the Castle Keep

desc:
You wander into a patch of shrubs dominated by dark gnarled trees whos warped
and twisted branches stretch all around, forming a canopy which blocks out all
external light. As you move, you step on old blackened leaves which crunch
loudly as you tread upon them and dark crows scatter from their shelter as you
approach.
name: Dark Clump of Trees

desc:
You wander into a patch of shrubs dominated by dark gnarled trees whos warped
and twisted branches stretch all around, forming a canopy which blocks out all
external light. As you move, you step on old blackened leaves which crunch
loudly as you tread upon them and dark crows scatter from their shelter as you
approach.
name: Dark Clump of Trees

desc:
Here is a makeshift kitchen for the preparation and cooking of food. There is a
crude oven, wood stacked beside it ready for burning. Several boxes and barrels
are also here, possibly the loot from some raid on unsuspecting travellers who
met their end against the current motley band who now control this fort.
name: The Kitchen and Stores
